$comment: Windy Translate ops surface — Class C, but an INTERNAL support service (Node on loopback:8099, consumed by windy-pro; not directly grandma-facing). Product API (/translate, /translate/batch, /detect) stays out per §2. Rev 2026-07-13 (gap-closing): (1) /version SHIPPED — MF1 compliant; (2) auth SHIPPED as an opt-in static bearer wall (install_token-style, fits a loopback service better than EPT): WINDY_TRANSLATE_TOKEN env, constant-time compare, gates everything EXCEPT /health + /version (probes and MF1 never depend on auth) — enforcement activates when the deploy env sets the token (Grant-gated); (3) get_logs SHIPPED — /ops/logs ring is content-free BY CONSTRUCTION (fixed event vocabulary + numeric/enum codes; raw worker errors are categorized, never stored, because tracebacks can embed the text being translated); (4) run_selftest SHIPPED — /ops/selftest canary-translates a fixed constant phrase through the real NLLB pipeline + SQLite round-trip, pass/fail per stage. DEPLOY STATUS: merged; live after the Grant-gated service restart — probe before trusting mid-incident.

get_health.note: rich: status + NLLB worker ready|loading + cache_size + uptime.

get_capabilities.note: supported language list — the honest capability probe for a translator.

get_logs.note: content-free by construction: fixed event vocabulary only — closed 2026-07-13.

run_selftest.note: canary phrase through the real worker + cache round-trip, per-stage results — closed 2026-07-13.

check_for_update.note: resolves the running version against admin's /v1/fleet-versions; status + remediation (Grant-gated restart). Closed 2026-07-13.

reset_to_defaults.note: holds the translation cache; clearing is a different, explicit action.

tools.name: get_health
tools.description: The FIRST call when translation is failing. Health of the Windy Translate service: whether the NLLB translation worker is ready or still loading, how big the cache is, and uptime. If the worker says 'loading', translations aren't ready yet.

tools.name: get_status
tools.description: Read Windy Translate's deployment identity: which build is actually serving right now (service name, package version, commit sha, when it started). Use this to tell whether a fix that was supposed to deploy is really live.

tools.name: get_capabilities
tools.description: List the languages Windy Translate can actually translate between right now. Use this when a user's target language 'isn't working' — it may simply not be in the supported set.

tools.name: get_logs
tools.description: Read Windy Translate's recent operational events — worker started, worker ready, worker crashed, translation errors by category (timeout / worker not ready / crashed), self-test results. Content-free by construction: the log can only ever hold fixed event names and codes, never any text that was translated.

entries.description: [{ts, level, event, code?}] — fixed vocabulary only

tools.name: run_selftest
tools.description: Actively exercise Windy Translate's core path: canary-translate a fixed test phrase through the real NLLB worker and round-trip the cache database. Returns pass/fail PER STAGE (worker / translate / cache) so you can see exactly where it broke — a loading model looks very different from a corrupt cache.

passed.description: true only when every stage passed ('passed', not 'ok' — top-level ok is reserved for the invoke envelope)

tools.name: get_cache_stats
tools.description: Read Windy Translate's cache statistics — how many translations are cached and how often each language is hit. A diagnostic for 'is caching working' and which languages are in demand. Counts only, no translated content.

tools.name: check_for_update
tools.description: Check whether a newer Windy Translate build is available. Resolves the running version against the fleet's version manifest and reports current, update-available, or must-update, with the literal next step when an update exists. Read-only — never deploys.
